page: Amazon Web Services m6id.xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with 4 vCPUs, derived from 2 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, the instance includes a bundled 237 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume, while network performance is anchored by a baseline bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate a mixture of average single-core capabilities and weaker multi-core scaling. The single-core stress-ng CPU performance scores 1890.49, and the Geekbench single-core score is 1649.0, both falling into the average category. However, multi-core metrics show limitations, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 3857.88 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 3488.0, both rated as weak. Memory bandwidth also trends lower, with large-block read and write speeds of 28.15 GB/sec and 24.59 GB/sec respectively.

In terms of specialized workloads, the CPU supports AVX-512 and cryptographic acceleration flags like AES and SHA_NI. This is reflected in average performance for CPU encryption tests and single-threaded gzip compression. Conversely, database operations and Redis SET performance are relatively weak. The instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for general-purpose workloads requiring fast local NVMe storage but is less suited for highly parallelized multi-threaded computing or memory-intensive database tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services m6id.xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 4 vCPUs and 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory, powered by an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz. Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, this x86_64 server includes a bundled 237 GB NVMe SSD local instance store and a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Benchmark data shows average single-core performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1649.0, but weaker multi-core scaling and memory bandwidth. The processor supports AVX-512 and cryptographic acceleration, yielding average results in encryption tests. Given its balanced resource profile and local NVMe storage, the m6id.xlarge is suited for general-purpose workloads, basic web serving, and lightweight caching applications that benefit from low-latency local storage, though it is less optimal for heavy multi-threaded processing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6id.xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 4 vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and a 237 GB NVMe SSD. It delivers balanced single-core performance for standard workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m6id.xlarge is an x86_64 general-purpose instance featuring 4 vCPUs, 16 GB DDR4 RAM, and a 237 GB local NVMe SSD.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processing and high-speed local NVMe SSD storage for low-latency workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8375C processor with 4 vCPUs and 16.0 GB DDR4 RAM
Bundled 237 GB NVMe SSD local instance store volume
Baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps on the AWS Nitro hypervisor
Average single-core performance with hardware-accelerated cryptography support
Suited for general-purpose applications, lightweight caching, and basic web serving
